---
title: "Custom Per-Cell Styled Table in Quarto PDF"
format:
  pdf:
    header-includes:
      - \usepackage{colortbl}   # enables \cellcolor
      - \usepackage{array}      # better column type control
      - \usepackage{xcolor}     # gray!XX color mixing
---


```{r}
#| echo: false
# ---- 1. Your data matrix -------------------------------------------------
data <- matrix(
  c("Q1", "120", "98", "White ok",
    "Q2", "145", "132", "Slight grow",
    "Q3", "110", "150", "Down",
    "Q4", "160", "170", "Up"),
  nrow = 4, byrow = TRUE
)
colnames(data) <- c("Period", "Target", "Actual", "Note")

# ---- 2. Style matrices, same shape as data -------------------------------
# background: "white" or "gray!20" (light grey) per cell
bg <- matrix(
  c("gray!20", "white",   "white",   "gray!20",
    "gray!20", "white",   "white",   "gray!20",
    "white",   "gray!30", "gray!30", "white",
    "white",   "white",   "gray!30", "white"),
  nrow = 4, byrow = TRUE
)

# font size (pt) per cell
size <- matrix(
  c(10, 10, 10, 9,
    10, 10, 10, 9,
    10, 10, 10, 9,
    10, 10, 10, 9),
  nrow = 4, byrow = TRUE
)

# alignment, ONE PER ROW (applies to every cell in that row)
row_align <- c("c", "c", "c", "l")

# minimum row height (pt), ONE PER ROW
row_height <- c(16, 22, 16, 26)

# border spec per cell: any combination of "T","B","L","R", or "" for none.
# Example below draws a full outer box plus a divider under row 1.
border <- matrix(
  c("TL",  "TB",  "TB",  "TR",
    "LB",  "TB",  "TB",  "RB",
    "L",   "",   "",   "R",
    "BL",  "B",  "B",  "BR"),
  nrow = 4, byrow = TRUE
)
```

```{r}
#| output: asis
#| echo: false

n_row <- nrow(data)
n_col <- ncol(data)

escape_latex <- function(x) {
  x <- gsub("&", "\\\\&", x)
  x <- gsub("%", "\\\\%", x)
  x <- gsub("_", "\\\\_", x)
  x
}

has_side <- function(spec, side) grepl(side, spec, fixed = TRUE)

# Builds one cell. `strut_pt`, if given, forces a minimum row height by
# inserting an invisible \rule of that height into the cell content.
build_cell <- function(val, color, pt, align, border_spec, strut_pt = NULL) {
  L <- has_side(border_spec, "L")
  R <- has_side(border_spec, "R")
  colspec <- paste0(if (L) "|" else "", align, if (R) "|" else "")
  strut <- if (!is.null(strut_pt)) sprintf("\\rule{0pt}{%dpt}", strut_pt) else ""
  sprintf(
    "\\multicolumn{1}{%s}{\\cellcolor{%s}\\fontsize{%d}{%d}\\selectfont %s%s}",
    colspec, color, pt, pt + 2, strut, escape_latex(val)
  )
}

# Turns a logical vector of "needs a rule here" per column into \hline or
# a set of \cline{a-b} segments (merging contiguous columns).
emit_line <- function(flags) {
  if (!any(flags)) return(NULL)
  if (all(flags)) return("\\hline")
  ncol <- length(flags); segs <- c(); i <- 1
  while (i <= ncol) {
    if (flags[i]) {
      j <- i
      while (j + 1 <= ncol && flags[j + 1]) j <- j + 1
      segs <- c(segs, sprintf("\\cline{%d-%d}", i, j))
      i <- j + 1
    } else {
      i <- i + 1
    }
  }
  paste(segs, collapse = "")
}

# ---- header row (unstyled border/height, just for column labels) --------
header_cells <- sapply(seq_len(n_col), function(j) {
  build_cell(colnames(data)[j], "gray!40", 10, "c", "")
})
header_line <- paste(paste(header_cells, collapse = " & "), "\\\\")

cat("\\begin{table}[h]\n\\centering\n")
cat(sprintf("\\begin{tabular}{%s}\n", paste(rep("l", n_col), collapse = "")))
cat("\\hline\n")
# cat(header_line, "\n\\hline\n")

for (i in seq_len(n_row)) {
  cells <- sapply(seq_len(n_col), function(j) {
    # only need to plant the height-forcing strut once per row (first cell
    # is enough — LaTeX rows are as tall as their tallest cell)
    strut <- if (j == 1) row_height[i] else NULL
    build_cell(data[i, j], bg[i, j], size[i, j], row_align[i], border[i, j], strut)
  })
  cat(paste(paste(cells, collapse = " & "), "\\\\"), "\n")

  # a rule appears below row i if row i asked for "B" OR the next row asked
  # for "T" at that column — this avoids drawing the line twice.
  bottom_flags <- sapply(seq_len(n_col), function(j) {
    this_b <- has_side(border[i, j], "B")
    next_t <- if (i < n_row) has_side(border[i + 1, j], "T") else FALSE
    this_b || next_t
  })
  ln <- emit_line(bottom_flags)
  if (!is.null(ln)) cat(ln, "\n")
}

# cat("\\hline\n\\end{tabular}\n\\end{table}\n")
cat("\n\\end{tabular}\n\\end{table}\n")
```
